title: Tender Briny Saudi Arabian Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Saudi Arabian dish featuring tender lamb in a rich and briny sauce, served with a side of liquid cheese and steamed in a papillote, perfect for a monsoon evening. This recipe combines the flavors of the Middle East with the comfort of a hearty stew.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the lamb pieces with salt, black pepper, cumin, and coriander.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium high heat.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the seasoned lamb pieces to the pot and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add 2 cups of water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Cook the lamb for 2 1/2 hours, or until it is tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and stir in the liquid cheese.
Wrap each serving of the lamb and sauce in a sheet of papillote paper and steam for 5 minutes.
